New Claude Code Hijacks Mouse Clicks in Your Terminal? Kill It with CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_MOUSE_CLICKS

When you're working in Claude Code and try to select text in the terminal or click a link in an error message, the clicks get hijacked by Claude Code itself. This post walks through the difference between CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_MOUSE and CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_MOUSE_CLICKS, how to add it to your .zshrc, and the one-off bypass hotkeys for iTerm2, macOS Terminal, and VS Code when you don't want to touch your config.
design.md: If Your AI-Generated UI Keeps Drifting, You're Missing Google's design.md

design.md is a format spec that Google Labs incubated from their AI design tool Stitch and then open-sourced. The goal is to make AI Coding tools like Claude Code, Cursor, and GitHub Copilot actually understand your design system. This post covers what design.md is, how it's different from rules files like CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md, what the mixed YAML Token + Markdown rationale structure looks like, a real example (Heritage), and what the built-in CLI can do (lint with WCAG contrast checks, diff, export to Tailwind v3/v4 or W3C DTCG, spec) — plus how to wire it into your AI Coding tool of choice.
Stats — The Free Open-Source macOS Menu Bar System Monitor I Use Every Day (CPU, GPU, Temperature, Network)

A walkthrough of Stats — the open-source macOS menu bar system monitor I run on every Mac I own. Covers what it shows (CPU, GPU, RAM, disk, network, battery, sensor temperatures, Bluetooth, multi-timezone clock, plus a Remote module), how to install it with `brew install --cask stats`, a quick tour through each module, the desktop widget trick, and a couple of macOS 26 widget quirks worth knowing about.
Make /llms.txt Return text/markdown on GitHub Pages: A Cloudflare Transform Rule Guide

I dropped /llms.txt onto my gh-pages site, ran curl -I, and found it coming back as text/plain. This post walks through fixing that with a single Cloudflare Response Header Transform Rule — no Worker needed, free plan covers it. Covers why .txt defaults to text/plain via mime-db, the free plan's 10-rule allowance, the full Dashboard setup, how to write the match expression, and verifying with curl -I.
Is AI Getting Quietly Dumber? AIStupidLevel: A 24-Hour Watchdog for LLM Degradation

This post walks through AIStupidLevel — a third-party benchmark site that keeps a 24-hour eye on 21 production AI models across 7 providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, xAI, DeepSeek, Kimi, GLM). It throws coding, reasoning, and tool-calling tasks at them every few hours, runs each task 5 times to take the median with a 95% confidence interval, and uses the CUSUM change-point detection algorithm to catch when a model is quietly getting dumber. We'll cover why models degrade (silent updates, quantization), how the four test suites work, how to read the Stupid Meter, and the bundled Smart Router that auto-picks the best model for you.
How Much of Your Post Does AI Search Actually Grab? Breaking Down Claude's WebSearch and WebFetch

This post digs into how much of your blog AI actually pulls in when it does a web search. I went through Anthropic's official web_search and web_fetch tool specs, broke down the fact that the search stage only returns title + URL, the 150-character cited_text cap, and Claude Code's built-in WebFetch using Haiku 3.5 to summarize a 100 KB truncation, then ran a real test against my own blog. Wraps up with four actionable AEO writing tips: titles that carry their own context, lead with the answer, design single sentences that can be quoted standalone, and use structure as navigation markers.
How to Use Gemini Omni Video Generation: 30 Prompt Templates for Reels, Product Demos, and Logo Reveals

After Google launched Gemini Omni at I/O 2026 in May, video generation in the Gemini App got a generational swap — it replaced Veo 3.1, with 10-second clips, native synced audio, and Chinese text rendering all coming together. This post first clears up how Omni in the Gemini App and Veo 3.1 on the developer side actually split the work, then walks through what makes Omni strong and how to write prompts that hold up, and ends with 30 scenario-specific prompt templates — IG Reels hooks, product demos, Logo Reveals, Cinematic B-roll, Before-After transitions, quote cards, and Lifestyle / travel / food cuts — all copy-paste ready.
Is Claude Code Auto Memory Hogging Your Memory? A Cleanup & Diagnostics Guide

Opened `~/.claude/projects/` and noticed it keeps growing? The first instinct is usually to blame Auto Memory. Turns out Auto Memory doesn't eat your RAM at all — it actually eats Context Window (MEMORY.md loads only the first 200 lines or 25KB) and disk space. And the real culprit bloating that folder isn't memory files, it's conversation jsonl. This post walks through what Auto Memory actually consumes, the `cleanupPeriodDays` auto-cleanup mechanism, `CLAUDE_CODE_SKIP_PROMPT_HISTORY` for fully disabling transcript writes, and how to use `find` to clean out large files manually.
What Are Claude Code Hooks? Auto-Run Lint, Block rm -rf, Inject Context on SessionStart — A Complete Guide

When you're developing with Claude Code, do you find yourself constantly telling it to run Prettier again? Or worrying that one day it'll accidentally fire off rm -rf? This post walks through how Claude Code Hooks work — when events like PreToolUse, PostToolUse, UserPromptSubmit, SessionStart, Notification, and Stop fire, how to set up matcher and command in settings.json, how to block actions with exit code 2 — and runs through practical hooks: auto-format, blocking rm -rf, reinjecting project context after Compact, Mac desktop notifications, plus prompt / agent hook types and /hooks debugging.
Can a Blog Really Make Tens of Thousands of Dollars a Month? Real Google AdSense Numbers, Explained

This post is about whether blogging actually makes money. I start with my own blog's three revenue sources (Google AdSense, Buy Me A Coffee, Portaly) at roughly an 8:1:1 split, then zoom in on Google AdSense. Covers CPM vs. CPC, how AdBlock kills impression revenue, my Google Analytics monthly active users, the AdSense performance report (page views, impressions, clicks, CPC, page RPM), how to calculate actual monthly earnings, and finally my own ad placement strategy and why I try to balance revenue against reader experience.
